title: 360-degree video coding using face-based geometry padding
abstract: A frame-packed picture for a 360-degree video content may be received, A group of continuous faces in the frame-packed picture may be identified based on frame packing information for the frame-packed picture. A sample Iocation in the group of continuous faces may be identified. Whether a neighboring sample Iocation associated with the identified sample location is located outside of a discontinuous edge of the group of continuous faces may be determined. If the neighboring sample iocation is located outside of the discontinuous edge of the group of continuous faces, geometry padding on the identified sample Iocation may be performed, if the neighboring sample Iocation is located outside of the discontinuous edge of the group of continuous faces, geometry padding may be skipped. The 360-degree video content may be processed based on the geometry padding.
